subject: UX & Research
title: The Facebook News Feed Backlash
description: One-minute skill: The Facebook News Feed Backlash.

hook.text: In 2006, Facebook launched the News Feed. Users called it creepy and formed protest groups with 700,000 members.

buildup.text: Before the Feed, you had to visit each friend's profile. The Feed surfaced activity automatically. People felt watched.

discovery.text: Mark Zuckerberg apologized but kept the feature. Within weeks, users loved it. The Feed became Facebook's core.

twist.text: The backlash was about surprise, not the feature itself. Users weren't prepared for the privacy shift.

climax.text: Introducing features that change privacy expectations requires gradual rollout and clear communication first.

punchline.text: Great features still need great introductions. Don't surprise users.

quiz.question: Why did users initially hate Facebook's News Feed?
options: It surfaced activity automatically, making users feel surveilled without warning
It slowed down the Facebook app
